title: Language-pair termbase (preserved terms, units, transliteration) for first target language
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual requires a language-pair termbase to guide translation and ensure clinical terms, units, negations, and urgency markers are handled consistently and correctly. Mistranslating negations or urgency is a safety-critical error. The termbase is a prerequisite for translate-001 and must be reviewed by the qualified translation reviewer (translators-001) before translation work begins.
objective: Create a language-pair termbase for the first target language covering: preserved terms (terms that should remain in source language or be transliterated), clinical units, negation patterns, urgency/consent markers, and required transliteration conventions. Obtain translation reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: glossary/termbase/<lang>-termbase.yaml (or equivalent) is created for the first target language, covering: preserved terms (terms to retain in source language or transliterate), clinical units, negation and urgency patterns, consent-term conventions, and transliteration rules if applicable
Termbase is reviewed and signed off by the qualified translation reviewer (translators-001)
Termbase entries are structured so translate-001 can reference them during translation (machine-readable and human-readable)
Termbase carries source attribution and a not-medical-advice note; license is source-compatible
No patient data or identifiable information is included
resources: PLAN.md — Solution approach Stage B section
output: glossary/termbase/<lang>-termbase.yaml for the first target language, reviewed and signed off by the translation reviewer
